title: mentha longifolia subsp. grisella
intro: Mentha longifolia subsp. grisella, also known as Horse Mint or Grisel's Mint, is a subspecies of the Mentha longifolia plant, belonging to the Lamiaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its ability to act as a natural antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant, making it effective in relieving digestive issues and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Mentha longifolia subsp. grisella include its ability to soothe digestive issues, such as indigestion and nausea, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of the common cold. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it, especially for medicinal purposes.
